solution: 1. Shut off water supply. 2. Remove old faucet and clean deck. 3. Insert mounting hardware from below. 4. Connect supply lines (cold/hot). 5. Secure with mounting nut. 6. Attach pull-out hose to spout. 7. Turn water on and check for leaks. Allow 24 hours for sealant to cure.

1. Place sink upside down on counter and trace outline. 2. Cut along inner line with jigsaw. 3. Apply silicone sealant on rim. 4. Lower sink into cutout. 5. Clamp from below. 6. Wipe excess sealant. 7. Connect drain and faucet. Do not use abrasive cleaners on granite composite.

Leaks are often from loose connections or worn O-rings. Check: (1) Supply line connections under sink - tighten with wrench. (2) Spout base - ensure mounting nut is tight. (3) Pull-out hose connection at spout. (4) Replace cartridge if water drips from spout when off. Use Carysil replacement cartridge part# LX200-C.

Clean with mild soap and soft cloth. Avoid bleach, ammonia, or abrasive pads. For stains use baking soda paste. Rinse and dry. Use cutting board to protect surface. Do not leave acidic liquids (lemon, vinegar) for long periods. Apply mineral oil occasionally to maintain finish.

1. Mark ceiling/wall position. 2. Run supply lines to location. 3. Install mounting bracket. 4. Connect shower arm to supply. 5. Attach rain shower head. 6. Seal with thread tape. 7. Test flow and adjust angle. For concealed installation, coordinate with plumber during rough-in.

1. Locate wall studs. 2. Install wall bracket at correct height (typically 32-36 inches to rim). 3. Connect waste and supply (in-wall or surface). 4. Hang basin on bracket and secure. 5. Connect mixer tap and drain. 6. Seal joints. Ensure wall can support basin weight (check specs).
